methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Xtend is a Series B-stage defense and security company that has raised $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private peers at the same stage and sector, supplemented by 2 public defense contractors (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, anchoring on $100M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $190M. The peer-median method surveyed 8 same-stage, same-sector companies including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, and Castelion, yielding a median valuation of $1.1B across a range of $300M to $1.5B. These two approaches reflect materially different assessments of Xtend's relative maturity and market position.

Synthesizing both methods yields a triangulated range of $162M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a median of $658M. The wide dispersion—spanning nearly one order of magnitude—reflects substantial uncertainty. The stage-progression method appears conservative relative to peer valuations, suggesting either that Xtend is less mature than typical Series B entrants in this sector or that comparable-company multiples have inflated beyond historical progression rates. Resolution depends on Xtend's revenue scale, customer concentration, and technical defensibility relative to the named peer set.
